actual.review_text:
This came up in a recent project we worked on.

A business owner was dealing with THOUSANDS of new records every day for cold email campaigns.

Out of that, they needed to reliably SHORT-LIST THE TOP 600 RELEVANT PROSPECTS before outreach went live.

They asked AI to do the filtering.

THE PROBLEM WASN’T SPEED.
IT WAS CONSISTENCY.

Some days the AI selected 580 records.
Other days it crossed 700.

Occasionally, it surfaced leads that clearly didn’t match previous campaigns.

SAME DATA.
SAME SETUP.
DIFFERENT RESULTS.

That’s where AI quietly becomes a BUSINESS RISK.

Their first instinct was to switch models, add more examples, or manually double-check the output.

Instead, we paused and looked at the task itself.

The AI wasn’t failing because it lacked intelligence.
It was failing because it wasn’t given CLEAR RESPONSIBILITY.

It didn’t know:

 → what must be excluded
 → which criteria mattered more
 → how to behave when the data didn’t clearly fit

Once those boundaries were defined, the output stabilized.

NOT BECAUSE THE AI GOT SMARTER,
BUT BECAUSE THE DECISION WAS FINALLY WELL-SCOPED.

When AI is used for REPEATABLE BUSINESS DECISIONS,
RELIABILITY MATTERS MORE THAN CREATIVITY.

If this sounds familiar, COMMENT “AI” and I’ll connect to understand what kind of inconsistency you’re seeing.

#AIinBusiness #ArtificialIntelligence #BusinessAutomation 
#Founders #Entrepreneurship #SalesOperations #GoToMarket

You’ve probably already validated this with AI.

It sounds right. 
It looks reasonable. 
It even feels convincing.

And that’s exactly why this stage is risky 
before engineering decisions get locked in.

AI is great at giving confident answers. 
It doesn’t show you what breaks if it’s wrong 
or how expensive that mistake could become later.

I stay close to technical challenges almost every day.

That means seeing 
where plans hold up, 
where assumptions fall apart, 
and where complexity quietly creeps in.

Before anything gets locked in, 
I spend time talking with founders 
to keep a human perspective in the loop 
before engineering decisions are made.

This isn’t about execution. 
It’s about judgment before commitment.

DM me and we’ll break it down.

#founders #entrepreneurship #startuplife #businessdecisions #productstrategy #techstrategy #scalingbusiness

As founders in tech, we often face uncertainty in our development journeys. I've found that structured planning is essential to navigate these challenges effectively. Here’s my approach to build planning that has served me well:

1. **Identify the Core Problem**: Clearly define the challenge we're addressing. Ensuring the entire team aligns on this vision is crucial for success.
2. **Prioritize the MVP**: Focus on the must-have features that deliver immediate value. It’s about understanding what truly matters to our users.
3. **Utilize Automation**: Tools like n8n and Zapier can streamline our processes, allowing us to concentrate on impactful work.
4. **Embrace Flexibility**: After launch, be ready to pivot based on user feedback. Iteration is vital for refining our product.

Remember, it’s not just about moving quickly; it’s about building with clarity and purpose. If you're navigating similar challenges, I’d love to connect and share insights! #TechDevelopment #Entrepreneurship #Automation

What is most likely to change after launch?

→ pricing rules
→ user roles and permissions
→ edge cases in workflows
→ usage volume or scale

These changes are what expose weak assumptions.

If a system isn’t designed with change in mind,
every update becomes risky and expensive.

Most underestimation doesn’t come from building the first version.
It comes from not planning for the first change.

prediction.review_text:
After launching an AI-driven solution, the real challenge often begins: effective execution. 🚀 It’s crucial to focus on the operational complexities that can derail our efforts post-launch. I've seen too many projects stumble because we didn't anticipate the adjustments needed for real-world scenarios. 

Here’s how I tackle these challenges:  
1. **Plan for Change**: Always have a proactive strategy ready to adapt to the evolving landscape.  
2. **Monitor Outcomes**: Establish clear metrics that guide our decisions and keep us aligned with our goals.  
3. **Engage Your Team**: Foster open communication to address issues collaboratively as they arise.  

Let’s commit to not just building systems that function, but ones that truly excel in delivering value! 💪 #AI #Automation #TechStrategy #Execution #CommunityEngagement

actual.review_text:
AI speeds up the first version.
It slows down the first real change.

Teams use AI to scaffold code and ship quickly.
The initial release looks fine.

Then something concrete changes:

→ pricing logic needs an update
→ a new user role needs access
→ one workflow behaves differently for a customer

That’s when execution stalls.
Not because development is slow,

but because no one is fully sure:
 
→ why certain decisions were made
→ what parts are safe to modify
→ what will break if they touch it

AI reduced the cost of getting to version one.
It didn’t reduce the cost of understanding the system.

Execution problems don’t appear at launch.
They appear at the first unavoidable change.

The end of a year always brings a different kind of honesty.

Some plans worked. Some didn’t.
That’s normal. What matters is who you became while navigating both.

This year wasn’t linear. It didn’t unfold exactly the way I expected.

Some things moved faster than planned. Others took longer and demanded patience.

A lot of the progress happened off screen. In decisions. In corrections. In showing up on days when energy wasn’t high.

Looking back, the growth wasn’t just in the outcomes.
It was in how I learned to handle uncertainty.

How I slowed down when clarity was needed.

How I continued when direction wasn’t perfect.
As 2026 begins, I am choosing to carry forward the lessons, not the pressure.

There is time. There is room. And there is movement, even when it is quiet.
Onward.

actual.review_text:
There’s a stage in every growing business where things don’t break…
they just stop flowing.

It’s subtle at first.
Nothing is “wrong,” but everything feels slightly slower than it should be.

And that friction usually shows up in small but costly ways:

→ CRM gets updated, but accounting doesn’t reflect it
→ Support resolves an issue, but sales never sees the context
→ Marketing generates leads, but operations doesn’t get the data
→ A project moves forward, but the CRM still shows it as pending

Nothing is broken.
But nothing is truly connected either.

And that’s when businesses start losing momentum.

Not because they lack automation.
But because their systems aren’t aligned.

This is why the earliest, highest-ROI automation investment isn’t AI or complex workflows.

It’s something far simpler:

Connecting the tools you already use so the business operates as one system, not five.

When CRM, accounting, support, marketing and operations stay in sync:

⤷ Work moves faster
⤷ Context stays clean
⤷ Decisions become clearer
⤷ Real automation finally becomes possible

Sometimes automation doesn’t start with replacing work.
It starts with connecting what already works.

 Curious whether your tools can actually sync cleanly or if alignment is the real bottleneck?

actual.review_text:
We’re seeing this a lot in the automation space, and honestly, I’ve been guilty of it too.

Most automation agencies make one mistake, they try to sell automation.
But selling automation often adds cost before it adds value.

Because in the early stages, most of what automation promises can actually be done manually, faster, cheaper, and with more context.

The real need for automation begins when manual work becomes the bottleneck, when scaling becomes painful, repetitive, and error-prone.

That’s when automation stops being a “nice-to-have” and becomes an enabler of growth.

So instead of trying to sell automation, we build it where the pain is real, where teams are already doing the work, and scaling it manually no longer makes sense.

At CodingRippler, that’s how we think about automation,
not as a product to push, but as a capability that emerges naturally when scaling requires it.

Maybe automation shouldn’t be sold, it should be discovered.

 Curious if your team has reached the point where automation actually makes sense?
Let’s explore that together → [

We automate invoice processing in 14-21 days. Most agencies quote 4-6 months.

If you're a CFO or Finance Head at a 10-100 person company, you know this pain:

→ Your team manually enters 200+ invoices/month from Gmail into Sheets
→ Vendors complain about late payments
→ You've thought about hiring another accountant, but that just scales the problem
→ Big consulting firms quote $150K and 6 months for "enterprise solutions" you don't need

Here's what we build (custom, in 2-3 weeks):

Gmail → AI extraction → Google Sheets using n8n workflows:

 Monitors your inbox 24/7 for invoice attachments
 Extracts vendor, amount, due date, line items (PDFs, images, screenshots)
 Validates against your business rules
 Logs to your Google Sheet structure
 Alerts your team based on your approval thresholds

Not a template. Built for how your finance team actually works.

Why 2-3 weeks instead of months?

Our team includes n8n implementation specialists who know the platform inside-out. We focus on your edge cases and approval workflows—not reinventing the wheel.

You get: working system + documentation + 30 days hands-on support.

Our clients typically see: 
→ 15+ hours/week saved per finance team member
→ Zero missed payment deadlines
→ Finance team refocused on actual financial planning

See how businesses have benefited from working with us: 
If your finance team is drowning: DM me a sample invoice (blur confidential info).

 I'll tell you in 24 hours if we can automate it and what it'll take.

actual.review_text:
Why SMEs Get Stuck in Hiring Hell

You know that moment when you finally decide to automate that tedious process?

You're excited, ready to move forward, and then you hit the wall of options.

Hiring full-time feels crazy for a month-long project. You'd spend longer recruiting than the actual work takes.

So you turn to freelancer platforms. Post your job, pick someone good, get it done.

Except suddenly you're staring at 50+ proposals.

Here's what you're really looking at: 

→ 30% copy-paste templates
→ 25% suspiciously under/overpriced
→ 20% zero relevant experience
→ 15% agencies pretending to be individuals
→ 10% might actually be worth your time

Half of which look suspiciously similar, and you're supposed to somehow figure out who can actually deliver what they promise.

This is the SME technical implementation trap.

Full-time hiring? Too expensive for short projects. Freelancer platforms? Too chaotic and unpredictable.

That's why I'm bringing India's top technical talents under CodingRippler - teams who understand the modern digital landscape, clear communication, predictable delivery.

Don't just take my word for it → [

We don't work 24/7. But our young technical minds treat every project as a chance to learn and grow. Your business challenges become their expertise.

When it's your lunch break, it's prime time in India. And when something's broken for your business, they take personal ownership to get it fixed.

This is where Indian technical talent truly shines:

→ When requirements change mid-project, they adapt without complaints
→ They iterate until it works perfectly, not just "good enough" 
→ Problem-solving that goes beyond the immediate fix

actual.review_text:
Why Small to Medium Startup Founders Need Agency Partners, Not Just Freelancers

After starting my agency, I'm observing a consistent pattern: small to medium startup founders choose freelancers first, then come to agencies to fix what went wrong.

"Where's my developer? They went silent for 3 days." "The code works, but no one documented anything." "We launched, but there's a critical bug and they're unavailable."

Individual talent isn't enough. Systems matter.

This realization hit me and my partners during our time at Microsoft, where we saw that even brilliant individual contributors needed systematic processes and team structures to deliver consistent enterprise-level results. That's exactly why we started this agency - because while traditional agencies exist, few understand the unique challenges of today's fast-moving digital business landscape.

The True Cost Reality: Freelancers may charge $20-25/hour, similar to our Indian agency rate of $25/hour, but that's just the visible cost. Factor in project delays, bug fixes, communication gaps, and hiring replacement developers - and that "cheaper" freelancer often costs 2-3x more than the initial estimate.
When you work with an agency, you're not just hiring one person. You're accessing:

A Complete Team Structure:
Senior developers who architect the solution
Junior developers who handle implementation efficiently
Project managers who ensure deadlines are met
QA specialists who catch issues before you see them

Built-in Accountability: When something goes wrong, there's always someone responsible for fixing it. No "sorry, I'm traveling" or "that's not my area of expertise."

Scalable Resources: Need to speed up development? We add resources. Hit a technical roadblock? We have specialists. Developer gets sick? Project continues without delay.

Long-term Partnership: We're invested in your success because our reputation depends on your results.

At comparable hourly rates, you get systematic delivery, consistent quality, and complete peace of mind - without the hidden costs that turn budget projects into expensive nightmares.

What's been your experience with freelancers vs agencies?

Ready to experience the agency advantage? Reach out to contact@codingrippler.com to see how we can help transform your next project.

How to Make Systems That Let You Work from Anywhere

A lot of business owners think working from anywhere means you have to do everything on your own.

Let’s stop that thinking.

Some people think that:

• You need fancy tools
• You must always be online
• You have to be available every minute
• You need to be everywhere

Yes, tools help a bit, but here’s the real way to work from anywhere:

• Have clear jobs for your team
• Automate things that take too much time
• Keep your services simple
• Follow up with clients
• Make things easy for clients
• Keep your business organized

These are the real steps to freedom.

Without them, you end up answering emails all day.

You’re always on your phone or computer.

Clients keep asking, “Are you free now?”

(I'm looking at you, busy business owners.)

Stop trying to do everything.
Make systems that let you do less.

When you do, you will have clients come to you, not the other way around.

Have you ever felt stuck because you are always working?

actual.review_text:
Celebrating 5 Years at Microsoft 

If you had told me when I graduated from WVU in 2014 with a Bachelor of Arts in Criminology that I would one day be a Senior Software Engineer at Microsoft with a Master of Science in Software Engineering, I might not have believed you. Yet here I am in 2025, reflecting on an incredible journey filled with growth, opportunity, and gratitude.

First, I owe so much to GEICO’s Veterans IT Development Program, which gave me my start in programming and changed the trajectory of my career. A heartfelt thanks also to Deloitte, where I learned invaluable soft skills and the true power of an inclusive and collaborative organization.

I’m deeply grateful to the West Virginia Army National Guard for enabling me to earn my master’s degree debt-free through their exceptional tuition assistance programs. If you’re looking for the best deal in education, this is it!

My time as a commissioned Field Artillery Officer in the Army National Guard has taught me leadership and resilience in ways that no classroom or corporate training ever could.

To all the incredible civilian and military leaders and mentors I’ve had the privilege of learning from over the years—your guidance and support have truly shaped who I am today. Your impact on my life has been profound, and I am forever grateful.

This journey is a testament to the power of hard work and having clear goals. It truly is the American dream.

I’m incredibly thankful, humbled, and blessed to be where I am today. Here’s to the next chapter!

#Microsoft #SoftwareEngineering #ArmyNationalGuard #Gratitude #AmericanDream

actual.review_text:
A new IndabaXEgypt webinar coming next week 
 
When: Saturday 27th March, 5pm Cairo time
Title: "Hardware Support for AI and Vice Versa"
Speaker: Prof.Mohamed Zahran
Registration link: 

This talk is unique as it will be the first IndabaXEgypt webinar around hardware support for AI applications and we are we lucky to have Prof. Mohamed Zahran to explore this space with us. Mohamed Zahran is a professor with the Computer Science Department, Courant Institute of Mathematical Sciences at New York University (NYU).

About the Talk:
As AI, especially ML, became a buzzword about a decade ago, a lot of research, both in industry and academia emerged to build better hardware to support it. In this talk, we will discuss several issues regarding AI and hardware design. We will try to answer the following questions. Why do we need specialized hardware for AI? What does it mean "better" hardware for AI? Can AI support hardware beside the traditional computer-aided-design tools? How will AI evolve and how will this affect the hardware? During the talk, we will raise questions as much as we give answers.

 #machinelearning #ai #artificialintelligence #deeplearning

actual.review_text:
Janet Yellen, who has just been nominated by President-Elect Joe Biden for Secretary of the Treasury, received an honorary Doctor of Commercial Science degree from President John Sexton during NYU's 2014 Commencement ceremony at Yankee Stadium. In her address to that year's graduating class, she spoke about the importance of grit and having the courage to take a stand:

"There is an unfortunate myth that success is mainly determined by something called 'ability.' But research indicates that our best measures of these qualities are unreliable predictors of performance in academics or employment. Psychologist Angela Lee Duckworth says that what really matters is a quality she calls 'grit'—an abiding commitment to work hard toward long-range goals and to persevere through the setbacks that come along the way.

"One aspect of grit that I think is particularly important is the willingness to take a stand when circumstances demand it. Such circumstances may not be all that frequent, but in every life, there will be crucial moments when having the courage to stand up for what you believe will be immensely important."

Photo credit: ©Gallo: Courtesy of NYU Photo Bureau.

actual.review_text:
Went through my LinkedIn feed today for a bit to see what's going on, and one thing caught my eye again: people keep posting about millionaires and billionaires and their alleged life/work routines, and in some way or another asking "what are you doing to become one of them?"

With that, my message is for the people who do not wake up at 6am every day to exercise, who do not take a cold plunge and then meditate before work, who have not read another 3 tech books in the past 2 months, who have not received 2 new certificates for online courses this week, who do not want to teach everyone "how to do things the right way" on social media platforms, who do not have a GitHub commit history that could impress, who did not drop out of school and start a company in their garage, who did not attend the super-fancy AI conference last month, who are not constantly super-motivated and self-driven at work, who do not have all the answers to "what's next", who do not regularly post their own picture along with a message of wisdom, ... 

It is for the people who, under the overwhelming influence of social media, and particularly, LinkedIn, think that they are not doing very well, that they could do better, that there is an easy path to success, whatever that means, that some people take that path without much effort, but they cannot even find it. It is for the people who prefer some quiet time with a cup of tea to another open source contribution, or a short walk outside instead of watching the keynote of BestDeveloperConference (made up name), who prefer spending time with family and kids to vibe coding and building that one project on a side after work, who feel that although they are at a good enough spot in their life, it could have been much better, and they could have been happier, ...

And the message is ... don't worry, you're doing just fine. Big part of what you see on LinkedIn is a lie, and another big part is just a filtered, idealized perspective that is meant to impress and attract attention. No one writes about the people who dropped out of school and started their company in their garage which failed in a few months, and then they couldn't get a job without proper education, and then some of them became homeless, and so on. That is not inspiring or motivating, so why post about it?! A small part that is the truth is still presented under a wrong light, because most of the time even successful people are given (and take, too) full credit for their success, whereas luck (the coincidental alignment of number of circumstances and events in a way that proved beneficial to their success) almost always should be given at least half of the credit.

If all these fancy posts make you feel bad about yourself, just stop using social media and live a happier life. Otherwise, as George Orwell once wrote, "ignorance is strength" (pun intended).

Sometimes you don’t cℎoose leadership… leadership cℎooses you.

Yesterday, I had a “life-changing” synergy session (aka coffee) witℎ myself and realized tℎat success is not about working ℎard — it’s about working smart… but also working hard… but smarter. You know wℎat I mean.

In just three days, I managed to empower cross-functional innovation by sending an email with two bullet points — and ℎonestly, tℎe impact ℎas been notℎing sℎort of transformational.

People keep asking me: “How do you stay so driven?”
Tℎe secret is simple: fail fast, iterate faster, and occasionally nap strategically.

Let’s remember: it’s not about tℎe destination OR tℎe journey.
 It’s about tℎe narrative you craft on LinkedIn afterward.

actual.review_text:
I love books, and I love reading some of them. It is not an easy problem to pick the next one to read and to know that I will like it even before starting. This is when ChatGPT comes to help, and it gives surprisingly good recommendations: 

I realized following people's suggestion does not always work, because what is good for me means something else from what is good for others. But what the good books for me have in common is objective and for LLMs it's just a set of numbers, and the relationship between those numbers is not based on taste, it's based on similarity (defined by some math formula). Long story short, I realized LLM's recommendation is much more reliable than human's when it comes to subjective opinions/taste. Give it a try!

Now, I am also giving you my own subjective suggestions if you like reading:
1. The Stranger, Albert Camus - how people perceive what is important, how easily society spreads biases, and more important topics
2. The Foundation series, Isaac Asimov - tons of thought-provoking and original ideas, exciting turns of events, and of course brilliant sci-fi
3. The Gospel according to Jesus Christ, Jose Saramago - another perspective on religion and Christianity
4. The grapes of wrath, John Steinbeck - great depression in the US, what it meant to a lot of families
5. Why fish don’t exist, Lulu Miller - some history about Stanford University founders and leaders, with surprising/eye-opening facts

Xfinity (aka Comcast) has been causing so much trouble with my Internet connection. The price constantly goes up, and the quality regularly goes down. Today it was particularly terrible; I've been losing connection every 5 mins in the evening and every ~20 earlier during the day.

I know if I complain, they will use any potential reason to blame me for the issues (as it happened many times already): not their modem, not their router, connection to device not wired, etc. The only thing I didn't verify yet, is whether the connection was bad at the input to my Wi-Fi router, or my router is the problem (very unlikely, since it had worked fine for years). Hence, I decided to do that verification, too, before calling Xfinity.

I wanted a script to check the connection and log any interruptions. I will connect a machine with an ethernet cable directly to my modem and run that script for a day or two, and then check the logs for issues.

Long story short, I just want to show how useful the AI assistants (ChatGpt, Copilot) can be. Here's what I got in 2 mins; will start my testing tomorrow:

actual.review_text:
Amazon is shutting down dpreview.com. This was an amazing resource for people who were looking to buy photo and video equipment (cameras and lenses), where one could see professional reviews with a bunch of samples and also a very detailed specification for each piece of hardware. One could also compare different items of the same category and search for comparison reviews.

This is one bad way for such a useful platform to disappear, especially considering that it was not Amazon's homegrown product, but an acquisition. But, putting it mildly, profit is the main metric nowadays, not the usefulness.

Another good initiative was smile.amazon.com, which donated a small percentage of what customers spent on Amazon.com to a charity of their choice. Again, very good idea, but I guess zero profit (although tax experts might disagree here), so they shut it down earlier in February.

R.I.P. dpreview

actual.review_text:
Random idea for reducing waste:

Imagine going on Amazon retail website (or any other eCommerce giant's), ordering something, and in shipping details picking an option "Ship in original box" or "Do not package". This could be eligible for majority of items that are already packaged in their factory boxes. So then Amazon would just print the shipping label on the original box and send it out. Maybe I am missing something, but I think as long as the label is on a flat surface and legible, the color of the box shouldn't matter. Imagine how many millions of cardboard boxes this will save.

I am sure today millions of people just open the Amazon box (the brown cardboard one), take out the item in its original box, and then get rid of the shipping box (hopefully everyone recycles, but who knows). So not sure what extra role that extra box plays other than taking more space, wasting more effort, and taking a lot of trees down. Yeah, I know, there is privacy, extra protection, etc. but those are minor, plus that's why I say the website should allow both choices with and w/o extra packaging.

Now finally, some items come in non-cardboard/plastic packaging, which is recyclable only in special facilities, and most of these end up in landfills. Even better reason for providing this option.

Յուրաքանչյուր անհատի օգնությունը շատ կարևոր է այս գործին ավելի մեծ ընթացք տալու և ծրագրերն ավելի էֆեկտիվ իրագործելու համար։ Կարող եք օժանդակել թե ֆինանսապես, և թե ձեր մասնագիտական հմտություններով։

Եթե կասկածներ ունեք ծրագրերի, կամ ընդհանրապես նախաձեռնության վերաբերյալ, SASTIC-ի անդամները մեծ հաճույքով կպատասխանեն ձեր հարցերին․ ։

Հուսով եմ՝ պատերազմի օրերին արտահայտված համախմբվածությունը և հայրենիքի զարգացմանն ուղղված գործունեությունը գոնե որոշ չափով կարտահայտվի նաև այստեղ։

actual.review_text:
Found this LinkedIn Insight about "career athleticism" and looked at my own path.
The first thing I'm struggling with is: where does the investment in one's learning lie? At many points in my career I noticed that I wanted to do more, learned in my private time, got more pay, but the skills I got weren't needed, so I had to change my workplace to apply the new skillset. I would've preferred to just stick to the same workplace and develop myself there. There's an old expression that says something along the lines of: "Everyone has knowledge someone else paid for." The portfolio approach makes an assumption that all skills can be acquired outside of a job, which is false. Also if a higher investment in building the portfolio is made, it will be factored into the asked salary. Another thing means that the gig itself would make its way into the employee's portfolio, would any company accept these details to be public?
The second challenge is building teams. Working in software development with gig programmers sounds a lot like consultancy, and most of the time the code quality and maintainability suffers.
Anyway that's my view on the data based candidate search.

I've just launched my first software engineering book, and I wanted to share this great news to all of you!

actual.review_text:
Title: From Clicking to Being Clicked: A Journey of Pride and Inspiration

Last week, as I embarked on a journey from Hyderabad to Pune in my Skoda Slavia sedan, little did I know that a simple stop for lunch would turn into a heartwarming encounter that would leave me inspired.

As I pulled into a hotel for a quick bite, I noticed two young boys admiring my car, their faces illuminated with awe as they snapped pictures. Stepping out after my meal, I caught them in the act, their initial excitement replaced by apprehension at being caught. But instead of shooing them away, I approached with a smile, inviting them to continue capturing the moment.

To my surprise, they confessed that they weren't just enamored by the sleek lines of my car; they were also inspired by the possibility of owning a similar one someday. Their enthusiasm was infectious, and soon their family members joined in, sharing in the joy of the moment and capturing memories with my car.
It was a poignant reminder of my own journey. I couldn't help but reminisce about my college days when I eagerly posed for a picture with someone else's car, dreaming of the day when I'd have one of my own. Now, as others stood beside my car, expressing their aspirations, I felt a surge of pride and gratitude.
This encounter served as a powerful reminder that dreams are within reach for those who dare to chase them. It's a testament to the transformative power of ambition and hard work. As I drove away, I carried with me not only the memories of that encounter but also a renewed sense of purpose.

A big thanks goes to Ubisoft Romania (Bucharest studio) for having me work on such a masterpiece game. I want to extend this appreciation to my manager at that time, Andrei-Claudiu Maracineanu-Manolescu for believing in me since the first day. You have my sincere appreciation.

My tenure at Ubisoft was essential for my career growth.
Thank you for having me be part of the journey.

P.S: If you've completed the game, you can find my name in the credits section dedicated to the Bucharest Ubisoft studio.

I've successfully completed the learning path "Become a C++ Developer" from LinkedIn Learning, a learning path which consists of over 17 hours of C++ theory and in-depth exercises!

My favorite module of the learning path was and still is "Web Servers and APIs Using C++", by Troy Miles.

The module was outstanding, I've learnt how to create and automate Web Servers with C++, deploy resources using Docker, include HTML5, CSS3 and PHP elements and, last but not least, integrate external resources such as MongoDB to the Web Servers.

I'm very grateful for everything that I've learnt and this is just the start!

I recommend the learning path "Become a C++ Developer" for every C++ passionate programmer and for everyone else who would like to enrichen their C++ knowledge.

Link to the LinkedIn Learning Path: 

Thank you, everyone!

#developer #docker #programming #cplusplus #objectorientedprogramming #linkedinlearning

actual.review_text:
As an international student who came to the US in 2008 and who benefited from the quality of education here, I have an advice for those who were thinking of coming to US for higher education any time soon (this year and 2021) - DO NOT come to the US for your higher education. There are 2 reasons for this that are straightforward:

(1) Quality of education on any online tool is subpar as compared to the on campus experience. Most classes are now being conducted online. I will be honest - it’s not worth paying the kind of money international students pay in tuition. There is no question that US has some of the best faculty but still the quality of online education would make you question what you are paying for. Several US higher education institutions today have impressive buildings and campuses, in many cases funded by higher tuition charged to international students. But, if you are not able to get any benefit from it, then what’s the point. 

(2) Several companies in US are not willing to hire international students with visa requirements. It’s likely there will be more visa restrictions imposed in this economy which will also limit your ability to find an internship or job. If you are paying upwards of 50000$ for a degree, you need to have a plan to pay that massive loan.

You’re invited to my first TEDx Talk and the first ever TEDxRowan University!

Join us this Friday, April 25th from 3–6 PM at the Rohrer College of Business for an afternoon of bold ideas and inspiring stories.

Leaving Rowan in 2005 with no prospects and no network, it feels great to have such a crazy journey over the last 20 years. From startups to big banks, to defense and now at Microsoft. It's an honor to share some of the lessons I've learned about cultivating innovation and unlocking inner potential.

A huge thank you to the powerhouse team behind this event: Victoria Van, Aiden Tahmazian, Sapjah Zapotitla, Elizabeth Pennington, and Michael T. Dominik, Ph.D. T. Dominik, Ph.D. — your vision and dedication made this possible.

I’m thrilled to share that People.ai today announced $100 million in Series D #funding at a $1.1 billion post-money valuation. 
 
This series was co-led by Mike Dinsdale, CFA Dinsdale of Akkadian Ventures and Abdulla AlBanna of Mubadala Capital, with participation from existing investors including ICONIQ Capital and Lightspeed Venture Partners.
 
Five years into our journey, I’m proud to say that we’ve built the industry’s premiere SmartData platform to power the world’s best sales account and opportunity management. 

I’ve shared some additional thoughts about the road People.ai has traveled and where we’re heading in my latest blog post:

Thank you to the entire People.ai team, past and present, that helped us get to this point. I also want to express appreciation to the Legendary CMOs and CROs who have been so generous in sharing their wisdom on our Legends of Sales and Marketing podcast. And most importantly, thank you to all People.ai customers. You have my commitment that People.ai will continue to put your needs first as we deliver innovative solutions that drive meaningful business impact. 

P.S. There’s no better way to get to know what we’ve been up to than to download PeopleGlass.app. It’s a modern spreadsheet-like CRM interface that you’ll love. Best of all, it’s free and it will save you at least 5 hours per week of Salesforce data entry. :)

actual.review_text:
Resilience...

That was the word I chose for myself back in January as my compass for navigating this year with strength, care, and purpose. I never imagined how deeply it would resonate today.

This week has been incredibly difficult. Many talented, passionate, and kind colleagues were impacted by layoffs. While I was somehow fortunate to remain, I carry a heavy heart for those who didn’t. I want you to know that I see you. I admire your courage. And I believe in your next chapter.

Resilience isn’t about pretending things are okay. It is about honoring the hard moments, learning from them and still choosing to move forward. If you need a connection, a conversation, or just someone to listen, I’m here.

To everyone navigating uncertainty right now: you are not alone. You are valued. And you are resilient.

#Resilience #SupportEachOther #MentalHealthMatters

actual.review_text:
Yesterday I had the honor and pleasure of being a panelist in a University of Washington (UW) event promoted by SOMOS.tech (We are tech) and Information School at UW.
We discussed about professional journey, and shared our interview insights and tips with the students who attended the event. 
 
I was very impressed and happy to see the engagement and enthusiasm of the students. They asked thoughtful and relevant questions and showed a genuine interest in learning more about how to pursue a career in this exciting and fast-growing area. 

I was also delighted to connect with my fellow panelists, Audrey Long, specialist in security @ Microsoft, and Victoria Wang, Product Manager @ Microsoft. who are amazing professionals and role models in their respective areas of expertise.

I enjoyed sharing some interview tips that I learned in my career. These tips can help anyone who is looking for their ideal job. Here are some of them:

- Be yourself and honest during interviews. Focus on your strengths and eagerness to learn and grow.

- Do more than classes and college assignments to stand out. Show your passion and curiosity, collaborate with your colleagues in your own and their projects.
 
- Don't be scared of joining hackathons if you don't know the techs involved. Join them to learn and connect with people. You don't need to be an expert, you just need to be open-minded and willing to learn.

- Be proud and confident of your identity and culture, whether it's LATINX or another. Diversity is a strength, not a weakness. Share your stories and experiences that make you who you are and what you value.

- Experiment with and create your own projects using AI technologies. This will help you learn faster and keep up to date. There are many tools and platforms that you can use to try AI. Your College probably have some Azure credits for students (as Audrey mentioned well). Solve real-world problems that matter to you, or just have fun with AI and see what you can make. 

- Ask ChatGPT or Copilot to review your resume or help you prepare for an interview.

- In an interview, show concrete examples of how you collaborate with your peers at College or at your internship. Talk about one or two examples, pick your best collaboration case. Highlight the outcomes of your collaboration, and how you helped the group learn and succeed.

- To avoid sounding arrogant, when talking about your achievements and what you did, start with what your group did together and then move to the points where you made a difference and helped the group learn and succeed. This will show your humility, initiative and teamwork skills.

Thank you Luz A Mendoza García, Maho Pacheco, Lindsay Sullivan and everyone involved in the organization of the event.

actual.review_text:
The way you communicate with your team is an important component to the team's success.
In the process of communicating better, there is one step called feedback that sometimes is understood as giving compliments about a good work done. 
Feedback is broader and more complex. An important perspective on giving feedback that is for many times avoided by people is the negative (or constructive) feedback. 
In a 2018's article, HBR brought to our attention a research in which it was identified that people tend to avoid colleagues who give them more negative feedbacks then the view they have on themselves. According to same research, in these cases, people look for other groups to reinforce the better view. 
Although agreeing on that, I still believe that giving negative feedback is an important mechanism in the communication of leaders and teams, as long as it gives an honest, constructive perception about what needs to be improved and the value that the person represents to the company.
So, when giving perspectives on someone's work, apply these steps:
1. Focus on the fact without judgements or subjective opinions. 
2. Show the impact on someone else or on the job itself.
3. Give suggestion on how to improve and talk about the value that the person represents to the team/company.

 Let's empower every people to innovate.

actual.review_text:
Com resultado de uma longa parceria entre o ELDORADO e a Microsoft, em 2017, o Instituto passou a ser a sede do único Microsoft Innovation Center (MIC) do estado de São Paulo.
O MIC Campinas conta com uma área exclusiva, de 42m², área de coworking, além de compartilhar os mais de 1.500 m² do ELDORADO. O espaço é ricamente equipado com dispositivos multimídia para áudio e videoconferência, Smart TVs, equipamentos modernos de projeção interativos e notebooks. 
Para dar suporte ao desenvolvimento e prototipagem, o MIC conta com kits de robótica, Surface Book, além de toda uma infraestrutura de equipamentos de foto e vídeo voltados à geração de conteúdos técnicos para a comunidade.
#ELDORADO20Anos #InspiraçãoPeloNovo #20AnosELDORADO #ELDORADO20 #historiadoELDORADO #pordentrodoELDORADO #institutoELDORADO

actual.review_text:
It’s official — I’m in the book 

When I first sat down for my interview with Izabela Duiwe, I didn’t get the questions in advance — she wanted my story to be as authentic as possible. I came in unprepared, and what followed was an honest, flowing conversation that brought back so many hidden memories I hadn’t realized were still with me.

In my chapter of The Women of Microsoft, I open up about the winding path I’ve taken as both a pianist and a software engineer — two passions that may seem worlds apart, but together taught me discipline, creativity, and resilience. Along the way, I share practical strategies on balancing multiple roles, managing time effectively, and protecting energy to avoid burnout. These reflections were built over years of perseverance, growth, and self-discovery.

This book is more than a publication — it’s a movement to celebrate the voices, journeys, and resilience of women in tech. Being part of this experience, alongside leaders whose journeys deeply inspired me, has been a real privilege. 

Huge thanks to Miri Rodriguez and Izabela Duiwe for bringing these stories to life! Launching today! Order your copy here: and I am looking forward to hear what you think! #microsoft #microsoft50 #WomenInTech

As both a software engineer and pianist, I’ve always believed that technology and creativity go hand in hand.

This book shares the journeys of incredible women who have shaped and led Microsoft, proving that passion, resilience, and innovation can break barriers. While I’m grateful to share my own story, what’s even more powerful is seeing the collective wisdom and leadership of so many inspiring women. Their experiences remind us of what’s possible when we lift each other up and push boundaries together.

Simon Sinek nails what it means to truly lead. He also nails what it means to truly love.

actual.review_text:
Earlier this week, I had the opportunity of interviewing with CNBC Squawk Box Asia to discuss some of our latest Work Trend Index findings. I’m excited for what’s around the corner with hybrid work and staying close with customers to learn more from how they continue to navigate some of the biggest challenges from the past year. Some major takeaways to think about :

Gen Z, women and underrepresented minorities have been most negatively affected in terms of career advancement, feeling engaged/excited about work, and getting a word in during meetings.

Hybrid work is going to be the new normal. 70% want flexible work to stay, while over 60% want more in-person time and more than 65% of leaders plan to redesign office space for hybrid work.

Hear more from the interview here: 
#HybridWork #FutureofWork #RemoteWork

As you see this is a public PDF created by SAP, so please feel free to download and share it.

actual.review_text:
Centralized Customer Payments for Headquarters and Subsidiaries by Komathi T SAP Labs India 

Welcome to the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Two-tier ERP blog series. 

In this blog, let’s look at centralized customer payments in a Two-tier deployment model.

S/4HANA customer payments is a public cloud solution delivered in the SAP Cloud Platform, which provides a self-service offering to enable our customers to deploy electronic bill presentment and payment processes. In this Two-tier scenario, where headquarters is running On-Premise (ECC6+ or S/4HANA On-premise) and the subsidiary is running on S/4HANA cloud, SAP S/4HANA cloud for customer payment serves as a centralized customer payment solution for both headquarter as well as subsidiary.

While running in such two-tier landscape, processing repetitive customer inquiries regarding invoice copies, underpayment, dispute cases, matching incoming payments with open items because of Time zone issues, Language barriers, Large numbers of documents would be a great challenge to the accounts team.

Check out the full blog here

actual.review_text:
Super pumped for Day 1 with AWS!
Its been an intense 3 years for me, but also an incredibly transformative time too. What began as an incredibly high stakes decision back in 2019 when I switched majors from MSEE to MSCS has finally paid off. I have been through so many job rejections that I’ve lost count.

In this post, I wanted to share my 2 cents about some major takeaways during my job hunt that I hope helps other job seekers in some way. 

There is one thing that I learned during this phase: you may face multiple rejections and it will most definitely hurt your motivation. But after a few days, you will recover, get over it, and keep going, so have faith in patience as it is the ultimate virtue in life! 

Do not get bogged down by rejections, pat yourself for a great attempt, figure out ways to enjoy your time through all this and practice strong mental health in any way you like (for me it was pranayam, gyming, cracking lame jokes with friends, traveling, gaming and playing my guitar) and trust that this rejection was necessary to set you up for the next attempt. Something that helped me reconcile with a rejection is a saying I heard a while back: “when competing for a job, you are essentially setup for failure”. You have no control over the outcome, you can only take pride in giving your best. In case of software interviews: prepare, execute, fail, identify MISTAKES, modify, repeat. It was necessary to fail, at least for me. 

I was terrible at data structures, algorithms and problem solving, and still have so much to learn, so to all my connections who are still hustling, keep hustling! I would be remiss not to give credit to all the LEGENDary friends (you know exactly who you all are) and family who helped me prepare and mentored me through the process. Kudos to Simran Bathla for a smooth onboarding and honestly being super dedicated at helping me click with the right team.

Cheers to new challenges, struggles and a ton of epic stuff to look forward to!

#amazon #aws #softwareengineer #newjob #newopportunities #newchallenges

actual.review_text:
Why I love paying taxes in USA and hate doing the same in India 
There is a public high school next to our home in NewJersey and it has world class sports facilities, science lab better than even what IITs or top engineering schools have in India and it’s alumni consist of nobel winners, olympic gold medallists and billionaire. It is free for anyone who lives in that area. Yeah you pay high property tax to live in such good school dist but it is worth every penny. 
On top of that you get world class roads, super safety and security and clean environment 
And after you retire Medicare insurance will give you best healthcare for rest of you life 

If you want to have such similar schools in india you need to pay atleast 1 lakh per month, live in high end expensive society and still you will not get such high end infrastructure or healthcare 
And on top of all that you need to pay 30 percent tax which won’t give you anything in return 
In Europe your taxes give you much more than usa and virtually everyone pays taxes while only 7 percent indian pay Income tax who are mostly salaried employees 
Isn’t it time to rethink about income tax in india and see we get back something in return for the hardearned money we pay #healthcare #incometax #education #infrastructure #India #usa

actual.review_text:
Dear LinkedIn family, I need your love, compassion, and generosity to help save my life. Unfortunately, for almost two years now, I’ve been privately fighting a very rare and difficult to treat cancer. But even after beating it twice, it has relapsed, and we’ve exhausted all the treatment options available in India. My oncologists have suggested me to urgently get to the US for further treatment where newer treatment options are available. However, the costs of these treatments are beyond the means of any average Indian family, and I need your help to raise the funds needed to get these treatments. Time is of grave essence as I’m deteriorating fast, and it is a matter of life and death for me. I request you to help us in any way possible as time is of grave essence. You can find further details on the crowdfunding page.

Institutes such as Harvard Medical School are researching newer treatments for my condition, including possible curative options. However, for an unsponsored international patient like me, the costs are prohibitively high, and we cannot afford this treatment without your help. For context, an average Indian’s annual salary would only be able to cover a single day (~$10,000) in the hospital let alone any treatment. All treatment costs, including those for any complications caused by these new treatments need to be paid by the patient and are collected in advance. My parents have already staked all their savings in this fight, and we need your help to make sure I do not lose this fight due to a lack of funds.

I request you to help us in any way possible, through contributions or just by just sharing this to someone who might be able to contribute to my medical treatment. Would also request you to further share this in your networks to help me raise the funds needed for my treatment. Every like and share matters and no contribution is too small. My life is at stake, and I’ll be forever grateful for your support in this difficult time. This will always be a social debt upon me which I will try my best to repay. 

Thank you and please find all further details on the fundraiser page. In case you have any further questions or suggestions, please feel free to reach me on sarthakfightscancer[at]gmail[dot]com and I will try to reply at the earliest.

 #help #medical #crowdfunding #fundraiser #cancer #cancertreatment #helpinghands #cancersucks #needhelp

Just finished the course “Learning React.js”! #reactjs
Made my first web app UI with React.

Look out for these key concepts:
Creating and rendering React elements,
Representing UI elements with React components,
adding props to display dynamic data,
Building components with JS class,
Using React states to re-render and change state of components,
bind event functions to UI elements with state variables,
and Conditional rendering of components.

JSX is a boon! Makes writing your own components much easier and makes my source much cleaner.

Eve Porcello does a great job of breaking down React concepts, and a hands-on example to help you build on your own.

actual.review_text:
Virtual Network Routing Appliance ("Routing Appliance") Public Preview!

During Ignite, I posted about exciting developments in building a high-performance, native appliance in Azure that will help customers achieve low-latency, high-throughput routing without the need of a Network Virtual Appliance (NVA). Today I'm happy to announce that we have released a public preview of this feature. I hope you get a chance to try it out and we are looking to build many more exciting capabilities on top of this new technology.

NVAs have been the backbone of complex Azure networking topologies where customers needed to route traffic through a central point. While there are still many use cases for NVAs, such as advanced firewalls, for many scenarios NVAs just added complexity but were required to provide a route point. NVAs require the deployment of a virtual machine, route traffic through the guest OS, require the additional overhead of OS management and can create a bottleneck in terms of performance.

Today, with the announcement of the Routing Appliance you get configurable "Azure native" route point with networking speeds up to 200Gbps. You get the management ease of any Azure resource. No need to deploy or configure an operating system. With just a few clicks and an Azure resource deployment, you have a high scale route enabled appliance with native support for Network Security Groups (NSGs) and User Defined Routes (UDRs) giving you easy traffic filtering and routing.

Read More: 

Thanks to Ashish Bhargava, Mario Lopez, Mohamed Hassan, Jackson Crewe, Nitin Goel, Pan Wang, Anshu Verma, Arjun Patel, Rishabh Tewari, Neeraj Motwani, Avijit Gupta, Damini Singal, Michal Zygmunt, Deepak Bansal

#azure #azurenetworking #microsoft

actual.review_text:
I was cleaning out old papers this weekend and ran across my first offer letter out of college. 

Today we see expectations of signing bonuses that would exceed the annual salary I was offered and stock grants that I could never have dreamed of. My signing bonus was $2,000. And 3,500 stock options. For those of you that are too young to remember what options were, it was the option to buy the stock at a given price. That was the strike price which was usually set the stock price on your start date. If the company had a share price of $10 when I started and 3 years later it's share price have gone to $20, I have the option to buy up to 3,500 shares at $10. That would have been a total profit of $35,000. Not bad but consider the fact that from day one of joining this company their share price never exceeded my strike price. So if I actually never saw a dime from my shares in this company.

To me it is a moment to remind everyone to be humble of what you have and how lucky you are to work in the industry you do. I chose this career out of a passion for computers and technology. I worry about the entitlement I see is starting to grow in this industry. Expectations of huge compensation packages without skill behind them. 

When I started at Metawave, I was hungry to learn every piece of technology I could. I worked in embedded systems and wrote c++ code to drive custom hardware. 64K of RAM and embedded microprocessor. I looked for the smartest guys in the company and spent every moment I could learning from them. 

if I can do another 20 years in this career my life will be fulfilled.

actual.review_text:
As I said in a previous post, today is exciting to have 2 great announcements. This is the second one, where I am so excited to see my sibling team release Network Security Perimeter (NSP) into General Availability! I have had the great privilege to work with this amazing team as they have built a complimentary feature to Azure Virtual Network Manager in the PaaS space. While not my direct team or product, NSP team is my closest peer and I've had the great opportunity to work with them on their design, share code, and build great ideas together. It's a great day to celebrate their achievement and bringing an amazing product to the Azure Portfolio.

Network security perimeter allows organizations to define a logical network isolation boundary for PaaS resources (for example, Azure Storage account and SQL Database server) that are deployed outside your organization’s virtual networks. It restricts public network access to PaaS resources within the perimeter; access can be exempted by using explicit access rules for public inbound and outbound. 

Features of a network security perimeter include: 
* Resource to resource access communication within perimeter members, preventing data exfiltration to non-authorized destinations. 
* External public access management with explicit rules for PaaS resources associated with the perimeter. 
* Access logs for audit and compliance. 
* Unified experience across PaaS resources. 

Learn more about NSP: 

One great example of using NSP is with Azure Monitor:

Today is a great day for managing your routing at scale in Azure! I'm happy to announce that my team is delivering the capability to manage your user-defined route tables at scale.

One of the complexities customers face with route tables in Azure is the management of linking them to multiple subnets and then managing the routing rules at a larger scale. With route management functionality coming into Azure Virtual Network Manager, customers can now simply create a single definition and apply it to hundreds of subnets with just a few clicks.

Additionally customers have asked for the capability to have more than the 400 rule limit that exists today. Customers who use AVNM for their route management will be able to create up to 1,000 rules in their route tables. This gives our customers the ability to create more complex and detailed route tables.

Great work by my team and I look forward to hearing how this feature helps your organization.

actual.review_text:
I would have never thought that creating a simple text prediction software is that easy. After watching one of the Veritasium videos on Markov chains I got inspired by the story how Markov himself created manual and tedios "network" based on the text of Eugene Onegin by Pushkin. I was suspicious that such a simple model with just checking last few charachers can produce text that, of course being non-sensical, seems very legit on the first glance.

So, I've spent an hour of so implementing this in C++, fed it with some English texts and I've got some really decent results, for example, looking like this:

"That wild yell seemed to come a violent puff of wind, and the door to the winding stair blew to with a shock that set the dust from the lintels flying. When I ran to push it open, I found that it was hopelessly fast. I was again a prisoner, and that if I wished it I could have no choice. The Count saw his victory in my bow, and his mastery in the trouble of my face, for he said almost joyously:, you don't count now; the Master is at hand. The attendant thinks it is some sudden form of religious mania at once might be angerous. I got a ladder myself, and crossing the wall, dropped down on the other side of a true love episode of which I know one side already...."

The C++ code is as simple as this 

Anyways, real LLMs are way more complex and they base their predicitons on words, or more preciely tokens and have multiple layers of decision makings. However, a simple code like this can still produce interesting results.

1db61e77-786d-4d4c-94e0-4dcc221af20b_rev.product_description: Reliability and consistency in AI decision-making

actual_review_data.review_text:
This came up in a recent project we worked on.

A business owner was dealing with THOUSANDS of new records every day for cold email campaigns.

Out of that, they needed to reliably SHORT-LIST THE TOP 600 RELEVANT PROSPECTS before outreach went live.

They asked AI to do the filtering.

THE PROBLEM WASN’T SPEED.
IT WAS CONSISTENCY.

Some days the AI selected 580 records.
Other days it crossed 700.

Occasionally, it surfaced leads that clearly didn’t match previous campaigns.

SAME DATA.
SAME SETUP.
DIFFERENT RESULTS.

That’s where AI quietly becomes a BUSINESS RISK.

Their first instinct was to switch models, add more examples, or manually double-check the output.

Instead, we paused and looked at the task itself.

The AI wasn’t failing because it lacked intelligence.
It was failing because it wasn’t given CLEAR RESPONSIBILITY.

It didn’t know:

 → what must be excluded
 → which criteria mattered more
 → how to behave when the data didn’t clearly fit

Once those boundaries were defined, the output stabilized.

NOT BECAUSE THE AI GOT SMARTER,
BUT BECAUSE THE DECISION WAS FINALLY WELL-SCOPED.

When AI is used for REPEATABLE BUSINESS DECISIONS,
RELIABILITY MATTERS MORE THAN CREATIVITY.

If this sounds familiar, COMMENT “AI” and I’ll connect to understand what kind of inconsistency you’re seeing.

#AIinBusiness #ArtificialIntelligence #BusinessAutomation 
#Founders #Entrepreneurship #SalesOperations #GoToMarket

You’ve probably already validated this with AI.

It sounds right. 
It looks reasonable. 
It even feels convincing.

And that’s exactly why this stage is risky 
before engineering decisions get locked in.

AI is great at giving confident answers. 
It doesn’t show you what breaks if it’s wrong 
or how expensive that mistake could become later.

I stay close to technical challenges almost every day.

That means seeing 
where plans hold up, 
where assumptions fall apart, 
and where complexity quietly creeps in.

Before anything gets locked in, 
I spend time talking with founders 
to keep a human perspective in the loop 
before engineering decisions are made.

This isn’t about execution. 
It’s about judgment before commitment.

DM me and we’ll break it down.

#founders #entrepreneurship #startuplife #businessdecisions #productstrategy #techstrategy #scalingbusiness

actual_review_data.review_text:
Before committing to a build, there’s one question worth answering:
What is most likely to change after launch?

In practice, it’s usually:

→ pricing rules
→ user roles and permissions
→ edge cases in workflows
→ usage volume or scale

These changes are what expose weak assumptions.

If a system isn’t designed with change in mind,
every update becomes risky and expensive.

Most underestimation doesn’t come from building the first version.
It comes from not planning for the first change.

AI speeds up the first version.
It slows down the first real change.

Teams use AI to scaffold code and ship quickly.
The initial release looks fine.

Then something concrete changes:

→ pricing logic needs an update
→ a new user role needs access
→ one workflow behaves differently for a customer

That’s when execution stalls.
Not because development is slow,

but because no one is fully sure:
 
→ why certain decisions were made
→ what parts are safe to modify
→ what will break if they touch it

AI reduced the cost of getting to version one.
It didn’t reduce the cost of understanding the system.

Execution problems don’t appear at launch.
They appear at the first unavoidable change.

How I slowed down when clarity was needed.

How I continued when direction wasn’t perfect.

There is time. There is room. And there is movement, even when it is quiet.
Onward.

actual_review_data.review_text:
We’re seeing this a lot in the automation space, and honestly, I’ve been guilty of it too.

Most automation agencies make one mistake, they try to sell automation.
But selling automation often adds cost before it adds value.

Because in the early stages, most of what automation promises can actually be done manually, faster, cheaper, and with more context.

The real need for automation begins when manual work becomes the bottleneck, when scaling becomes painful, repetitive, and error-prone.

That’s when automation stops being a “nice-to-have” and becomes an enabler of growth.

So instead of trying to sell automation, we build it where the pain is real, where teams are already doing the work, and scaling it manually no longer makes sense.

At CodingRippler, that’s how we think about automation,
not as a product to push, but as a capability that emerges naturally when scaling requires it.

Maybe automation shouldn’t be sold, it should be discovered.

 Logs to your Google Sheet structure
 Alerts your team based on your approval thresholds

Why 2-3 weeks instead of months?

Our team includes n8n implementation specialists who know the platform inside-out. We focus on your edge cases and approval workflows—not reinventing the wheel.

Why SMEs Get Stuck in Hiring Hell

You know that moment when you finally decide to automate that tedious process?

You're excited, ready to move forward, and then you hit the wall of options.

Hiring full-time feels crazy for a month-long project. You'd spend longer recruiting than the actual work takes.

So you turn to freelancer platforms. Post your job, pick someone good, get it done.

Except suddenly you're staring at 50+ proposals.

Here's what you're really looking at: 

→ 30% copy-paste templates
→ 25% suspiciously under/overpriced
→ 20% zero relevant experience
→ 15% agencies pretending to be individuals
→ 10% might actually be worth your time

Half of which look suspiciously similar, and you're supposed to somehow figure out who can actually deliver what they promise.

This is the SME technical implementation trap.

Full-time hiring? Too expensive for short projects. Freelancer platforms? Too chaotic and unpredictable.

That's why I'm bringing India's top technical talents under CodingRippler - teams who understand the modern digital landscape, clear communication, predictable delivery.

Don't just take my word for it → [

actual_review_data.review_text:
Why Small to Medium Startup Founders Need Agency Partners, Not Just Freelancers

After starting my agency, I'm observing a consistent pattern: small to medium startup founders choose freelancers first, then come to agencies to fix what went wrong.

"Where's my developer? They went silent for 3 days." "The code works, but no one documented anything." "We launched, but there's a critical bug and they're unavailable."

Individual talent isn't enough. Systems matter.

This realization hit me and my partners during our time at Microsoft, where we saw that even brilliant individual contributors needed systematic processes and team structures to deliver consistent enterprise-level results. That's exactly why we started this agency - because while traditional agencies exist, few understand the unique challenges of today's fast-moving digital business landscape.

The True Cost Reality: Freelancers may charge $20-25/hour, similar to our Indian agency rate of $25/hour, but that's just the visible cost. Factor in project delays, bug fixes, communication gaps, and hiring replacement developers - and that "cheaper" freelancer often costs 2-3x more than the initial estimate.
When you work with an agency, you're not just hiring one person. You're accessing:

A Complete Team Structure:
Senior developers who architect the solution
Junior developers who handle implementation efficiently
Project managers who ensure deadlines are met
QA specialists who catch issues before you see them

Built-in Accountability: When something goes wrong, there's always someone responsible for fixing it. No "sorry, I'm traveling" or "that's not my area of expertise."

Scalable Resources: Need to speed up development? We add resources. Hit a technical roadblock? We have specialists. Developer gets sick? Project continues without delay.

Long-term Partnership: We're invested in your success because our reputation depends on your results.

At comparable hourly rates, you get systematic delivery, consistent quality, and complete peace of mind - without the hidden costs that turn budget projects into expensive nightmares.

What's been your experience with freelancers vs agencies?

Ready to experience the agency advantage? Reach out to contact@codingrippler.com to see how we can help transform your next project.

They often ignore broken checkouts thinking it's not that big of a deal.

Here’s how I’d fix it if I started from scratch:

Map the Customer Journey – Understand the full journey from landing page to checkout. Identify friction points using user behavior tracking tools.

Add AI to Automate – With AI-powered systems, I can help businesses dynamically adjust their checkout process in real time, based on user behavior.

Simplify the Forms – Don’t ask for unnecessary details. Focus on what truly matters.

Test in Real-Time – Use automation to run A/B tests on payment methods, page layouts, and form fields to see what truly works.

Upsell and Cross-sell Smartly – Use AI-driven recommendations during checkout to increase the cart value without being intrusive.

Fixing your checkout doesn’t have to be hard. With AI-driven tools and smart automation, you can boost your conversions and save valuable time.

Celebrating 5 Years at Microsoft 

If you had told me when I graduated from WVU in 2014 with a Bachelor of Arts in Criminology that I would one day be a Senior Software Engineer at Microsoft with a Master of Science in Software Engineering, I might not have believed you. Yet here I am in 2025, reflecting on an incredible journey filled with growth, opportunity, and gratitude.

First, I owe so much to GEICO’s Veterans IT Development Program, which gave me my start in programming and changed the trajectory of my career. A heartfelt thanks also to Deloitte, where I learned invaluable soft skills and the true power of an inclusive and collaborative organization.

I’m deeply grateful to the West Virginia Army National Guard for enabling me to earn my master’s degree debt-free through their exceptional tuition assistance programs. If you’re looking for the best deal in education, this is it!

My time as a commissioned Field Artillery Officer in the Army National Guard has taught me leadership and resilience in ways that no classroom or corporate training ever could.

To all the incredible civilian and military leaders and mentors I’ve had the privilege of learning from over the years—your guidance and support have truly shaped who I am today. Your impact on my life has been profound, and I am forever grateful.

This journey is a testament to the power of hard work and having clear goals. It truly is the American dream.

I’m incredibly thankful, humbled, and blessed to be where I am today. Here’s to the next chapter!

#Microsoft #SoftwareEngineering #ArmyNationalGuard #Gratitude #AmericanDream

313325f0-0334-45f7-93b3-e65adcc597e9_rev.product_description: Webinars exploring hardware support for artificial intelligence

actual_review_data.review_text:
A new IndabaXEgypt webinar coming next week 
 
When: Saturday 27th March, 5pm Cairo time
Title: "Hardware Support for AI and Vice Versa"
Speaker: Prof.Mohamed Zahran
Registration link: 

This talk is unique as it will be the first IndabaXEgypt webinar around hardware support for AI applications and we are we lucky to have Prof. Mohamed Zahran to explore this space with us. Mohamed Zahran is a professor with the Computer Science Department, Courant Institute of Mathematical Sciences at New York University (NYU).

About the Talk:
As AI, especially ML, became a buzzword about a decade ago, a lot of research, both in industry and academia emerged to build better hardware to support it. In this talk, we will discuss several issues regarding AI and hardware design. We will try to answer the following questions. Why do we need specialized hardware for AI? What does it mean "better" hardware for AI? Can AI support hardware beside the traditional computer-aided-design tools? How will AI evolve and how will this affect the hardware? During the talk, we will raise questions as much as we give answers.

 #machinelearning #ai #artificialintelligence #deeplearning

313325f0-0334-45f7-93b3-e65adcc597e9_rev.product_description: Courage to persevere in the face of challenges

actual_review_data.review_text:
Janet Yellen, who has just been nominated by President-Elect Joe Biden for Secretary of the Treasury, received an honorary Doctor of Commercial Science degree from President John Sexton during NYU's 2014 Commencement ceremony at Yankee Stadium. In her address to that year's graduating class, she spoke about the importance of grit and having the courage to take a stand:

"There is an unfortunate myth that success is mainly determined by something called 'ability.' But research indicates that our best measures of these qualities are unreliable predictors of performance in academics or employment. Psychologist Angela Lee Duckworth says that what really matters is a quality she calls 'grit'—an abiding commitment to work hard toward long-range goals and to persevere through the setbacks that come along the way.

"One aspect of grit that I think is particularly important is the willingness to take a stand when circumstances demand it. Such circumstances may not be all that frequent, but in every life, there will be crucial moments when having the courage to stand up for what you believe will be immensely important."

Photo credit: ©Gallo: Courtesy of NYU Photo Bureau.

351405ab-deb1-45b5-810c-d32798c95f40_rev.product_description: Reflections on success in the context of social media

actual_review_data.review_text:
Went through my LinkedIn feed today for a bit to see what's going on, and one thing caught my eye again: people keep posting about millionaires and billionaires and their alleged life/work routines, and in some way or another asking "what are you doing to become one of them?"

With that, my message is for the people who do not wake up at 6am every day to exercise, who do not take a cold plunge and then meditate before work, who have not read another 3 tech books in the past 2 months, who have not received 2 new certificates for online courses this week, who do not want to teach everyone "how to do things the right way" on social media platforms, who do not have a GitHub commit history that could impress, who did not drop out of school and start a company in their garage, who did not attend the super-fancy AI conference last month, who are not constantly super-motivated and self-driven at work, who do not have all the answers to "what's next", who do not regularly post their own picture along with a message of wisdom, ... 

It is for the people who, under the overwhelming influence of social media, and particularly, LinkedIn, think that they are not doing very well, that they could do better, that there is an easy path to success, whatever that means, that some people take that path without much effort, but they cannot even find it. It is for the people who prefer some quiet time with a cup of tea to another open source contribution, or a short walk outside instead of watching the keynote of BestDeveloperConference (made up name), who prefer spending time with family and kids to vibe coding and building that one project on a side after work, who feel that although they are at a good enough spot in their life, it could have been much better, and they could have been happier, ...

And the message is ... don't worry, you're doing just fine. Big part of what you see on LinkedIn is a lie, and another big part is just a filtered, idealized perspective that is meant to impress and attract attention. No one writes about the people who dropped out of school and started their company in their garage which failed in a few months, and then they couldn't get a job without proper education, and then some of them became homeless, and so on. That is not inspiring or motivating, so why post about it?! A small part that is the truth is still presented under a wrong light, because most of the time even successful people are given (and take, too) full credit for their success, whereas luck (the coincidental alignment of number of circumstances and events in a way that proved beneficial to their success) almost always should be given at least half of the credit.

If all these fancy posts make you feel bad about yourself, just stop using social media and live a happier life. Otherwise, as George Orwell once wrote, "ignorance is strength" (pun intended).

351405ab-deb1-45b5-810c-d32798c95f40_rev.product_description: Using recommendations for personal reading choices

actual_review_data.review_text:
I love books, and I love reading some of them. It is not an easy problem to pick the next one to read and to know that I will like it even before starting. This is when ChatGPT comes to help, and it gives surprisingly good recommendations: 

I realized following people's suggestion does not always work, because what is good for me means something else from what is good for others. But what the good books for me have in common is objective and for LLMs it's just a set of numbers, and the relationship between those numbers is not based on taste, it's based on similarity (defined by some math formula). Long story short, I realized LLM's recommendation is much more reliable than human's when it comes to subjective opinions/taste. Give it a try!

Now, I am also giving you my own subjective suggestions if you like reading:
1. The Stranger, Albert Camus - how people perceive what is important, how easily society spreads biases, and more important topics
2. The Foundation series, Isaac Asimov - tons of thought-provoking and original ideas, exciting turns of events, and of course brilliant sci-fi
3. The Gospel according to Jesus Christ, Jose Saramago - another perspective on religion and Christianity
4. The grapes of wrath, John Steinbeck - great depression in the US, what it meant to a lot of families
5. Why fish don’t exist, Lulu Miller - some history about Stanford University founders and leaders, with surprising/eye-opening facts

actual_review_data.review_text:
Xfinity (aka Comcast) has been causing so much trouble with my Internet connection. The price constantly goes up, and the quality regularly goes down. Today it was particularly terrible; I've been losing connection every 5 mins in the evening and every ~20 earlier during the day.

I know if I complain, they will use any potential reason to blame me for the issues (as it happened many times already): not their modem, not their router, connection to device not wired, etc. The only thing I didn't verify yet, is whether the connection was bad at the input to my Wi-Fi router, or my router is the problem (very unlikely, since it had worked fine for years). Hence, I decided to do that verification, too, before calling Xfinity.

I wanted a script to check the connection and log any interruptions. I will connect a machine with an ethernet cable directly to my modem and run that script for a day or two, and then check the logs for issues.

Long story short, I just want to show how useful the AI assistants (ChatGpt, Copilot) can be. Here's what I got in 2 mins; will start my testing tomorrow:
